summary: Russian UAVs intensified attacks on Sumy region fuel stations, striking four sites in 48 hours. Ukraine and Russia exchanged 160 prisoners of war. Ukrainian forces reported increased FPV drone usage and ongoing drone attacks on infrastructure.
key_points: Russian Shahed‑type UAVs attacked four fuel stations in Sumy region within 48 hours, including strikes on June 24‑25, using single and paired drones (Source: @eRadarrua/98253).
Ukraine and Russia exchanged 160 prisoners of war, as reported by the Russian Defense Ministry (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31187).
Ukrainian commander Syrsky said Russia plans to raise FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by year‑end (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31193).
A Shahed drone hit the Kremenchuk oil refinery in Poltava region, according to the Russian MoD (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31189).
FPV drones attacked the Nikopol area, killing two and injuring twelve, including children (Source: @UkraineNow/82209).
Ukrainian forces destroyed a Russian BM‑21 “Grad” launcher near the settlement of Snagost using a 47th OMBr unit (Source: @DeepStateUA/23598).
Russian UAVs launched from the Black Sea towards southern Odesa, targeting Vylkove (Source: @kpszsu/66503).
A MiG‑31K equipped with X‑47M2 Kinzhal missiles took off from Savasleyka airfield (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68082).

sections.executive_summary: Russian Shahed UAVs have escalated attacks on Sumy’s fuel infrastructure, hitting four stations in two days. A prisoner exchange saw 160 captives returned by both sides. Ukraine reports a planned surge in FPV drone operations, while both sides continue targeting energy facilities.

points: Ukrainian forces reported the destruction of a Russian BM‑21 “Grad” multiple rocket launcher near the settlement of Snagost, credited to the 47th OMBr unit (Source: @DeepStateUA/23598).
A Shahed‑type UAV struck the Kremenchuk oil refinery in Poltava region, causing damage as noted by the Russian Ministry of Defense (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31189).
FPV drone attacks in the Nikopol area resulted in two civilian deaths and twelve injuries, including two 12‑year‑old girls (Source: @UkraineNow/82209).
Ukrainian authorities reported a successful interception of ballistic missiles aimed at Kyiv, stating all were downed (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68047).
Ukrainian troops engaged a Russian Shahed‑type UAV over the Kremenchuk hydroelectric dam, confirming the hit (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68068).
Russian Shahed‑type UAVs attacked four fuel stations in Sumy region within 48 hours, using single and paired drones, with strikes on June 24‑25 (Source: @eRadarrua/98253).
Russia announced a prisoner exchange involving 160 Ukrainian POWs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31187).
Russian forces launched UAVs from the Black Sea toward southern Odesa, heading to Vylkove (Source: @kpszsu/66503).
A MiG‑31K equipped with X‑47M2 Kinzhal missiles took off from Savasleyka airfield (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68082).
Russian air force employed FAB‑1500 aerial bombs on the Slavyanskaya TPP, targeting Ukrainian positions (Source: @tass_agency/382564).
Ukrainian forces destroyed a Russian BM‑21 “Grad” launcher near Snagost, indicating active combat in the eastern sector (Source: @DeepStateUA/23598).
FPV drone strikes in Nikopol (southern Dnipropetrovsk) caused civilian casualties, showing drone activity on the eastern flank (Source: @UkraineNow/82209).
Russian Shahed UAVs targeted the Kremenchuk oil refinery in Poltava, a strategic eastern logistics hub (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31189).
Ukrainian reports of intercepted ballistic missiles over Kyiv suggest defensive actions affecting the eastern approach (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68047).
Russian aerial bombing with FAB‑1500 on Slavyanskaya TPP indicates continued air strikes in the eastern industrial zone (Source: @tass_agency/382564).
Ukrainian sources reported UAV movements from Bryansk region toward Chernihiv, passing near Lyubech and Slavutych (Source: @kpszsu/66483).
Multiple Shahed‑type UAV launches were noted from the Gvardiyivskyi and Millerovo districts, threatening northern positions (Source: @eRadarrua/98251).
Ukrainian radar tracked a reactive UAV heading from Zaporizhzhia toward the north of Kharkiv, targeting Bogoduhiv (Source: @kpszsu/66476).
Ukrainian forces observed UAVs moving from the Black Sea northward toward Odesa, then to Vylkove, indicating cross‑regional activity (Source: @kpszsu/66503).
A Shahed UAV was sighted near the Kyiv‑Zhytomyr border region of Krasiatych, indicating northern incursions (Source: @eRadarrua/98276).
Russian Shahed UAVs attacked fuel stations in Sumy region, but also launched from the Black Sea toward southern Odesa, targeting Vylkove (Source: @kpszsu/66503).
Ukrainian reports of a Shahed strike on the Kremenchuk oil refinery in Poltava region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31189).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed hit on the Kremenchuk hydroelectric dam (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68068).
Russian aerial bombing with FAB‑1500 on Slavyanskaya TPP, a southern power plant, indicates continued pressure (Source: @tass_agency/382564).
Ukrainian statements mention increased FPV drone usage on the front lines, potentially affecting southern operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31193).
Russian Shahed‑type UAVs conducted multiple attacks on Sumy fuel stations, using both single and paired drones (Source: @eRadarrua/98253).
Ukrainian forces reported downing all ballistic missiles aimed at Kyiv, indicating effective air defence (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68047).
A MiG‑31K equipped with X‑47M2 Kinzhal missiles launched from Savasleyka airfield, showing advanced Russian air capabilities (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68082).
Russian air force employed FAB‑1500 aerial bombs on the Slavyanskaya TPP, targeting Ukrainian positions (Source: @tass_agency/382564).
Ukrainian radar tracked reactive UAVs moving from Zaporizhzhia toward Dnipropetrovsk, indicating ongoing UAV operations (Source: @kpszsu/66484).

key_developments: Russian Shahed UAVs attacked four fuel stations in Sumy region within 48 hours, using single and paired drones, causing fires and infrastructure damage (Source: @eRadarrua/98253).
Ukraine and Russia exchanged 160 prisoners of war, marking a significant humanitarian development (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31187).
Ukrainian commander Syrsky announced Russia plans to increase FPV drone sorties from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by year‑end (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31193).
A Shahed drone struck the Kremenchuk oil refinery in Poltava, confirming continued targeting of energy assets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31189).
FPV drone attacks in Nikopol killed two civilians and injured twelve, including children, highlighting civilian risk (Source: @UkraineNow/82209).
Ukrainian forces destroyed a Russian BM‑21 “Grad” launcher near Snagost, showing effective anti‑artillery action (Source: @DeepStateUA/23598).
A MiG‑31K with X‑47M2 Kinzhal missiles took off from Savasleyka, indicating deployment of advanced strike platforms (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68082).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian UAV activity, especially Shahed attacks on fuel infrastructure in Sumy and energy facilities in Poltava, demonstrates a focus on disrupting logistics and civilian morale. The planned surge in FPV drone usage suggests an escalation in low‑altitude strike capability, increasing risk to Ukrainian forward positions. Advanced Russian air assets, such as MiG‑31K equipped with Kinzhal missiles, raise the threat level for strategic targets across the theater.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian air defenses have successfully intercepted ballistic missiles targeting Kyiv, indicating robust defensive measures in the capital region. Local resistance in Sumy has reported multiple UAV strikes, prompting heightened alertness and potential reinforcement of fuel depot security. Internationally, the prisoner exchange of 160 individuals may ease some humanitarian pressures but does not alter the overall combat dynamics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Shahed launch sites in the Gvardiyivskyi and Millerovo districts is critical, as recent launches have targeted northern and eastern fronts. UAV flight paths from Bryansk toward Chernihiv suggest cross‑border reconnaissance that could precede ground operations. The increased FPV drone sortie rate forecasted by Russian command warrants expanded electronic warfare and counter‑UAV deployments on the front lines.
